title: Catching the irony
desc: Read tone and respond in kind
setting: You show up late, again. Roberto greets you with classic deadpan sarcasm. The skill: catch the irony and volley it back.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: ¡Pero qué puntual! Solo cuarenta minutos tarde.
steps.en: How punctual! Only forty minutes late.

options.es: Es que la puntualidad es lo mío, ya sabés.
options.en: Punctuality is my thing, you know.
options.feedback: You caught the sarcasm and played along — leaning INTO the joke is the witty move.

options.es: Perdón, de verdad lo siento mucho.
options.en: Sorry, I really am very sorry.
options.feedback: He's teasing, not scolding — earnest apology misreads the tone. Lighten up and volley back.
Defensiveness kills the joke. He's ribbing you affectionately — play, don't justify.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Ah, claro. Deberían darte un premio.
steps.en: Oh, sure. They should give you an award.

options.es: Ya lo tengo en casa, junto a los otros.
options.en: I've already got it at home, next to the others.
options.feedback: Brilliant — extending the bit with a deadpan add-on. That's native-level humor.

options.es: Jaja, sí, perdón por la tardanza.
options.en: Haha, yeah, sorry for being late.
options.feedback: The laugh shows you got it, but slipping into apology deflates the volley. Keep the bit going.
Calling out the exaggeration ends the game flatly. The fun is in escalating it together.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Jajaja, ya ni con vos se puede.
steps.en: Hahaha, can't even win with you anymore.

options.es: Aprendí del maestro del sarcasmo, ¿qué esperabas?
options.en: I learned from the master of sarcasm, what'd you expect?
options.feedback: A compliment wrapped in a comeback — peak Nica banter. You've fully arrived.

options.es: Tantos años de práctica con vos.
options.en: So many years of practice with you.
options.feedback: Warm, witty, and self-aware. The friendship and the humor both shine.
You can pivot, but cutting the fun short feels abrupt. Let the laugh land first.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Te volviste más nica que yo, maje.
steps.en: You've become more Nica than me, dude.

options.es: Alguien tenía que mantener el nivel por aquí.
options.en: Someone had to keep up the standard around here.
options.feedback: Cheeky and confident — capping the exchange on a perfect comedic high note.

options.es: Jaja, me lo tomo como un honor.
options.en: Haha, I'll take that as an honor.
options.feedback: Warm and genuine while staying playful — a lovely way to receive the praise.

options.es: Qué va, vos seguís siendo el rey.
options.feedback: Generous and humble with a wink — true camaraderie. Beautifully done.
